description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, gray stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped by a pitched wooden roof with reddish-brown, overlapping tiles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A wooden crank handle is attached to one post, and a rope descends into the well, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism. A wooden bucket sits beside the well, and small decorative elements like flowers and stones are placed at the base. The structure is robust and designed for outdoor use, providing both functional and aesthetic value in an urban or park setting.
description: A classic stone water well with a tiled wooden roof, crank handle, and bucket, surrounded by small decorative flowers and stones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's stone wall, tiled roof, crank handle, bucket, and decorative flowers at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the roof's triangular profile, the crank handle, and partial visibility of the bucket and flowers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the tiled roof, stone wall, and a glimpse of the bucket and crank mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's triangular end, bucket, stones, and flowers more prominently.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank handle with a rope and bucket is present, attached to one post.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well; wooden roof supported by two posts acts as a canopy.
attribute_decorative_elements: Small flowers and stones placed at the base of the well.
